text: Radar doesn't need light — it listens for radio echoes bouncing off objects. How does it work? Radar sends out pulses of radio waves, like invisible messengers. When these waves hit an object, they bounce back, creating echoes. By measuring the time it takes for echoes to return, radar calculates distance. This means radar can see through fog, revealing what’s ahead, even in zero visibility. Want to dive deeper into radar tech? Hit subscribe for more science secrets!
